warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose: The Fukui reactivity analysis for pyridine was executed using Density Functional Theory (DFT) at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (specified as tier "standard") with the PySCF backend. The molecular geometry was provided in the file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz`, representing a neutral singlet system (charge 0, multiplicity 1) in the gas phase. Mulliken population analysis was used to derive Fukui indices. The most electrophilic site corresponds to the nitrogen atom with f⁺ = 0.48423 (dimensionless). Converged values for f⁺, f⁻, and f⁰ charges summed to ~1.0 for each system, indicating stability; no non-convergence or unexpected drift was observed. The run integrity verified as trustworthy.
